title: Solar PV system costs
subtitle: Median residential solar photovoltaic (PV) costs in the United States, measured in real 2015 dollars. Installed non-module prices include hardware costs, such as inverters and racking equipment; and the wide assortment of soft costs, including such things as marketing and customer acquisition, system design, installation labor, permitting and inspection costs, and installer margins.
caption:
Solar PV system costs

Median residential solar photovoltaic (PV) costs in the United States, measured in real 2015 dollars. Installed non-module prices include hardware costs, such as inverters and racking equipment; and the wide assortment of soft costs, including such things as marketing and customer acquisition, system design, installation labor, permitting and inspection costs, and installer margins.

This line chart tracks median residential solar photovoltaic costs in the United States from 2000 to 2015, measured in real 2015 dollars. Three distinct lines represent different cost components: Total Installed Price, Non-Module Costs, and Module Price. The vertical axis ranges from $0 to $12. In 2000, the Total Installed Price begins near $11.50, while Non-Module Costs start around $7.50 and Module Price sits just under $4. All three lines show a general downward trend over the fifteen-year period. The Total Installed Price line remains the highest throughout, dropping below $10 around 2003 and finishing near $4 in 2015. The Non-Module Costs line fluctuates slightly between $5 and $6 from 2000 to 2008, then declines steadily to approximately $3.50 by 2015. The Module Price line stays relatively flat near $4 until 2008, then drops sharply, falling below $2 by 2010 and ending near $1 in 2015. The gap between Total Installed Price and the sum of the other two components narrows over time, particularly after 2008. By 2015, all three cost categories sit at their lowest points on the chart, with Module Price occupying the bottom position, Non-Module Costs in the middle, and Total Installed Price at the top, though all are clustered within the lower half of the vertical axis range.
